title: fix jsonl_to_markdown
body:
<!-- To ensure we can review your pull request promptly please complete this template entirely. -->

<!-- Please reference the issue number here. You can replace "Fixes" with "Closes" if it makes more sense. -->
Fixes #162

Changes proposed in this pull request:
<!-- Please list all changes/additions here. -->
- Add `jsonl_to_markdown.py` utility script to convert JSONL files to Markdown format
- Script extracts the 'text' field from each line in a JSONL file and saves it as a separate Markdown file
- Include proper error handling for JSON decoding and file operations
- Add example usage and documentation

## Before submitting

<!-- Please complete this checklist BEFORE submitting your PR to speed along the review process. -->
- [x] I've read and followed all steps in the [Making a pull request](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#making-a-pull-request) section of the `CONTRIBUTING` docs.
- [x] I've updated or added relevant docstrings following the syntax described in the [Writing docstrings](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#writing-docstrings) section of the `CONTRIBUTING` docs.
- [x] If this PR fixes a bug, I've added a test that will fail without my fix.
- [x] If this PR adds a new feature, I've added tests that sufficiently cover my new functionality.

fixes  #162

step_plan.summary: Harden the scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py utility to safely handle empty/malformed JSONL lines and varied 'text' types, produce deterministic/sanitized filenames, and expose a robust argparse-based CLI (main()). These changes improve resilience and add a testable return summary.

steps.operation: Harden jsonl_to_markdown to robustly parse JSONL and produce deterministic filenames
steps.reason: The existing implementation fails on empty/whitespace lines, non-dict JSON values, and non-string 'text' fields and produces non-deterministic simple filenames. Make the function tolerant of empty lines, malformed or non-dict JSON values, and varied 'text' types; produce stable, sanitized file names (zero-padded indices and optional id-based names) and return a summary for testing.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral changes to output filenames (zero-padded indices and id-based names) can break downstream tooling or tests that expect the old 'line_i.md' names; callers that relied on the original function's lack of a return value should be updated to use the returned summary dict. Implementation may require a pre-pass to count lines (slightly more I/O or memory if read into memory) to determine padding width; alternatively use a fixed padding width (6) to avoid two passes. Duplicate sanitized ids must be handled (e.g., include index suffix) to avoid collisions. Tests must be added/updated to assert the new filenames, skip/ error counts, and returned summary. No external API surface other than the function signature should change (maintain argument names and behavior where possible).

steps.operation: Add a proper CLI main() using argparse and wire it to jsonl_to_markdown, providing flags for common options
steps.reason: The repository had an ad-hoc __main__ block expecting exactly two args. Replace it with a main() that uses argparse to provide a predictable CLI experience (positional input_file and output_dir, plus flags like --aggregate, --overwrite, --skip-empty, and --fallback-keys). The main() should call jsonl_to_markdown, print a concise summary, and set appropriate exit codes on fatal errors so it can be used in scripts and tests reliably.
steps.side_effects: CLI behavior changes: scripts or CI that invoked the script relying on the previous exact-2-arg behavior may need slight updates; however, maintain backward-compatible positional arguments so most usages continue to work. New flags change UX and may introduce new code paths to be tested (aggregate mode, overwrite mode, skip-empty). Tests will need to exercise the new CLI options. Exit codes are now meaningful (0 success, non-zero on fatal IO/usage errors), which could affect callers expecting previous behavior.
